canonical_rows.title: Directors and Chief Executives
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: AI will compress analysis, planning, and coordination layers around this role, but final accountability and organizational leadership remain human-intensive.
canonical_rows.summary: Leads large enterprises, business units, and complex organizations across finance, manufacturing, technology, logistics, and services.
canonical_rows.task_line: Allocates capital, sets strategy, reviews performance, coordinates senior teams, and manages external relationships.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: AI can accelerate reporting, planning, and analysis, but the role still depends on judgment, accountability, and negotiation in the real world.
sample_postings: Business head for a multi-city retail operation with P&L ownership.
Chief operating role combining vendor management, compliance, and growth planning.

canonical_rows.title: Other Department Managers
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: The work contains a large digital coordination component, but execution still depends on human supervision, escalation handling, and relationships.
canonical_rows.summary: Runs operations, sales, HR, logistics, procurement, hospitality, and facility functions inside medium and large organizations.
canonical_rows.task_line: Turns strategy into staffing plans, vendor workflows, schedules, budgets, and team supervision.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: AI will automate reporting, scheduling, and workflow analysis, but a large share of the job still sits in people management and local execution.
sample_postings: Operations manager for a warehousing network across North India.
Category manager for consumer goods procurement and channel coordination.

canonical_rows.title: Computing Professionals
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: This is one of the most exposed occupation groups because core tasks are digital, decomposable, and already seeing large productivity gains from AI.
canonical_rows.summary: Includes software developers, systems designers, product engineers, and other fully digital computing occupations.
canonical_rows.task_line: Builds software, tests systems, writes code, documents workflows, and coordinates with product and operations teams.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: The work product is digital and increasingly assisted by coding copilots, code generation, QA automation, and agentic workflow tooling.
sample_postings: Full-stack developer for fintech APIs, internal dashboards, and release automation.
Platform engineer for cloud infrastructure, observability, and CI/CD reliability.

canonical_rows.title: Architects, Engineers and Related Professionals
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: AI can speed up analysis and documentation substantially, but engineering work still carries physical-world, safety, and regulatory constraints.
canonical_rows.summary: Covers civil, mechanical, industrial, electrical, and design engineers along with technical professionals working on plans, drawings, and systems.
canonical_rows.task_line: Creates specifications, estimates, technical documents, calculations, and project coordination artefacts.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: AI will accelerate drafting, simulation setup, documentation, and design iteration, but site constraints, compliance, and physical deployment limit full automation.
sample_postings: Civil design engineer for highways and urban drainage packages.
Industrial engineer for process optimization in automotive manufacturing.

canonical_rows.title: Health Professionals (except nursing)
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: AI will be highly useful in the information-processing parts of the job, but core care decisions and patient-facing work remain only moderately exposed.
canonical_rows.summary: Includes physicians, dentists, pharmacists, and other diagnosis-heavy clinical professionals outside nursing.
canonical_rows.task_line: Evaluates patients, interprets evidence, documents care, and makes treatment decisions under regulatory and safety constraints.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: AI will support diagnostics, documentation, triage, and administrative work, but direct care, liability, and contextual judgment remain major barriers.
sample_postings: General physician for an outpatient urban clinic with teleconsult support.
Hospital pharmacist managing dispensing, counselling, and stock compliance.

canonical_rows.title: Nursing Professionals
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: Nursing is only moderately exposed because AI helps the paperwork and monitoring layers, not the physical and interpersonal core of the occupation.
canonical_rows.summary: Delivers bedside care, monitoring, medication administration, patient counselling, and ward coordination across hospitals and clinics.
canonical_rows.task_line: Combines physical care, documentation, supervision, escalation, and continuous patient interaction.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: Documentation and care planning can be assisted by AI, but the job remains anchored in direct physical care and real-time human presence.
sample_postings: Staff nurse for intensive care and post-operative ward rotation.
Community health nurse for maternal and child outreach visits.

canonical_rows.title: Other Teaching Professionals
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: AI will reshape content creation and assessment heavily, but live teaching, student management, and trust still require human presence.
canonical_rows.summary: Includes school teachers, tutors, instructors, and other teaching roles outside higher education specialist tracks.
canonical_rows.task_line: Prepares lessons, explains concepts, evaluates work, manages classrooms, and interacts with parents or institutions.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: AI will automate lesson planning, grading support, and low-stakes explanation, but classroom management and social trust keep the role from becoming fully digital.
sample_postings: Secondary mathematics teacher for CBSE curriculum and exam preparation.
Spoken English instructor for an urban skills training institute.

canonical_rows.title: Business Professionals
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: Core outputs are text, spreadsheets, and analysis, so AI can automate or compress a significant portion of daily work.
canonical_rows.summary: Includes accountants, analysts, auditors, finance roles, HR specialists, and strategy-heavy office occupations.
canonical_rows.task_line: Produces reports, reconciliations, plans, policies, analyses, and stakeholder communications.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: A large share of the work is digital and document-centered, making it highly sensitive to language models and workflow automation.
sample_postings: Financial analyst for budgeting, variance analysis, and board-ready reporting.
HR business partner handling policy drafting, recruiting analytics, and MIS dashboards.

canonical_rows.title: Computer Associate Professionals
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: The role is not as exposed as software engineering, but a large share of repetitive troubleshooting and documentation is automatable.
canonical_rows.summary: Includes user-support technicians, system operators, and implementation-focused IT support roles.
canonical_rows.task_line: Resolves incidents, configures systems, supports users, monitors infrastructure, and documents fixes.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: Routine support, knowledge retrieval, and scripting work are increasingly handled by AI assistants and automation platforms.
sample_postings: L2 support engineer for network operations and user issue triage.
ERP implementation support role with ticket handling and documentation.

canonical_rows.title: Secretaries and Keyboard-Operating Clerks
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: The occupation is highly exposed because much of the work is routine digital coordination and document handling.
canonical_rows.summary: Performs office coordination, typing, scheduling, record maintenance, and administrative support work.
canonical_rows.task_line: Manages email, documents, calendars, filings, and repetitive office communications.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: Administrative text work and routine coordination are increasingly handled by AI-native office tooling and workflow automation.
sample_postings: Executive assistant handling scheduling, correspondence, and meeting notes.
Back-office operator for documentation, data entry, and workflow tracking.

canonical_rows.title: Numerical Clerks
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: Routine numerical processing and document-driven bookkeeping are among the most compressible office workflows.
canonical_rows.summary: Covers accounts clerks, payroll staff, billing operators, bookkeepers, and transaction-processing roles.
canonical_rows.task_line: Processes invoices, reconciles ledgers, updates payrolls, and records repetitive financial transactions.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: These workflows are highly structured and sit directly in the path of spreadsheet automation, OCR, and LLM-assisted accounting tooling.
sample_postings: Accounts executive for billing, GST entries, and vendor reconciliation.
Payroll clerk managing attendance imports and monthly salary processing.

canonical_rows.title: Client Information Clerks
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: A large part of first-line customer communication is structured and scriptable, making this group highly exposed.
canonical_rows.summary: Includes reception roles, customer support desks, call-center support, and front-office information handling.
canonical_rows.task_line: Answers queries, updates records, routes requests, and follows scripted service workflows.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: Voice bots, chat assistants, and automated workflows will absorb more first-line support, though escalation-heavy work will remain.
sample_postings: Customer support associate for omnichannel complaint resolution.
Front-office executive handling walk-in queries and CRM updates.

canonical_rows.title: House Keeping and Restaurant Services Workers
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: The administrative layer can be digitized, but the core service work still depends on physical presence and human interaction.
canonical_rows.summary: Includes waitstaff, room attendants, kitchen support, and hospitality service workers operating in physical venues.
canonical_rows.task_line: Delivers in-person service, cleaning, food support, guest assistance, and shift-based coordination.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: AI will help scheduling, ordering, and supervision, but the physical service work remains grounded in presence and dexterity.
sample_postings: Steward for table service and customer handling in a quick-service chain.
Housekeeping attendant for hotel rooms, linen, and guest requests.

canonical_rows.title: Personal Care Workers
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: This group has low exposure because the value comes from physical, relational, and continuous human care.
canonical_rows.summary: Includes home-care support, attendants, and direct personal assistance occupations.
canonical_rows.task_line: Helps people with mobility, hygiene, feeding, supervision, and other direct assistance tasks.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: AI can support documentation and scheduling, but the work itself is intimate, physical, and difficult to automate cheaply in India.
sample_postings: Elder care attendant for home visits and daily living support.
Patient care assistant for hospital ward mobility and hygiene support.

canonical_rows.title: Shop Salespersons and Demonstrators
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: Digital selling tools and automated recommendations will compress parts of the role, but a large in-person component remains.
canonical_rows.summary: Works in retail counters, showrooms, assisted commerce, and product demonstration roles.
canonical_rows.task_line: Interacts with customers, explains products, handles transactions, and maintains display or stock flow.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: AI will reshape product discovery, assisted selling, and CRM workflows, but store-floor interaction and local trust still matter.
sample_postings: Retail sales associate for consumer electronics with product demos.
Relationship sales executive for in-store apparel and assisted checkout.

canonical_rows.title: Market Gardeners and Crop Growers
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: AI can guide crop decisions but cannot cheaply replace large amounts of physical farm work in Indian conditions.
canonical_rows.summary: Covers crop growers working in market-oriented cultivation, farm operations, and seasonal production systems.
canonical_rows.task_line: Performs planning, sowing, irrigation, input use, field supervision, and harvest coordination.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: Digital advisory tools will improve decisions, but low mechanization, fragmented holdings, and physical fieldwork limit near-term AI substitution.
sample_postings: Farm supervisor for vegetable cultivation, irrigation schedules, and labor deployment.
Protected cultivation technician for seedling, fertigation, and harvest records.

canonical_rows.title: Market-Oriented Animal Producers and Related Workers
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: The occupation remains low exposure because the physical care tasks dominate and the economics favor augmentation over replacement.
canonical_rows.summary: Includes dairy, poultry, livestock, and other animal husbandry occupations tied to market production.
canonical_rows.task_line: Feeds animals, manages sheds, monitors health, handles breeding cycles, and coordinates supplies.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: Sensors and advisory systems can help monitoring, but direct care and low-cost manual labor remain central.
sample_postings: Dairy farm supervisor for feeding plans, milk records, and herd health tracking.
Poultry unit operator for shed operations and daily production logging.

canonical_rows.title: Market-Oriented Crop and Animal Producers
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: AI will mainly augment planning and advisory layers while leaving the core production work largely intact.
canonical_rows.summary: Represents mixed-farming households and workers combining crop and livestock production in market-oriented settings.
canonical_rows.task_line: Balances seasonal cultivation, livestock care, local procurement, and small-enterprise farm decisions.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: Information support can improve yields and planning, but labor remains dispersed, physical, and difficult to automate at low cost.
sample_postings: Mixed-farm operations lead for crop planning, cattle care, and local market dispatch.
Agri-extension role supporting input scheduling and farm record collection.

canonical_rows.title: Building Frame and Related Trades Workers
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: The work is highly physical and site-specific, so AI exposure remains low.
canonical_rows.summary: Includes masons, structural workers, and building-frame trades working on civil and construction sites.
canonical_rows.task_line: Measures, cuts, lays, lifts, and assembles materials in changing site conditions.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: Design tools and site planning can improve, but most value sits in physical execution with weather, material, and safety constraints.
sample_postings: Shuttering carpenter for high-rise concrete formwork.
Mason for residential and commercial structural works.

canonical_rows.title: Building Finishers and Related Trades Workers
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: AI affects estimation and supervision more than the hands-on finishing work, so exposure stays low to moderate.
canonical_rows.summary: Includes plasterers, tilers, painters, and finishing trades that work after structural build-out.
canonical_rows.task_line: Handles detailed physical finishing, adjustments, surface prep, and on-site client coordination.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: Planning and estimation will digitize further, but the actual trade work remains manual and location-specific.
sample_postings: Tile fixer for interior finishing on commercial fit-outs.
Construction painter for surface preparation and coating work.

canonical_rows.title: Machinery Mechanics and Fitters
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: AI will improve diagnosis and maintenance planning, but the occupation still depends heavily on physical repair work.
canonical_rows.summary: Maintains industrial machinery, production equipment, and mechanical systems in workshops and factories.
canonical_rows.task_line: Inspects machines, diagnoses faults, replaces parts, aligns assemblies, and keeps production lines running.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: Predictive maintenance and diagnostics will improve with AI, but physical repair, troubleshooting, and local constraints remain central.
sample_postings: Maintenance fitter for FMCG packaging lines and breakdown response.
Workshop mechanic for pumps, gearboxes, and rotating equipment.

canonical_rows.title: Electrical and Electronic Equipment Mechanics and Fitters
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: Compared with purely mechanical trades, the digital diagnostic layer is larger, but the occupation still cannot be done entirely from a computer.
canonical_rows.summary: Works on electrical systems, instrumentation, panels, devices, and field repairs across industrial and service settings.
canonical_rows.task_line: Tests circuits, installs equipment, traces faults, and carries out physical maintenance on live systems.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: Remote diagnostics and smart-device monitoring will reduce some routine work, but installations and repairs remain physical and safety-critical.
sample_postings: Electrical maintenance technician for plant utilities and MCC panels.
Field service engineer for electronics installations and device diagnostics.

canonical_rows.title: Textile, Garment and Related Trades Workers
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: The work is repetitive enough to face automation pressure, but labor-cost economics and physical handling keep exposure moderate.
canonical_rows.summary: Includes sewing-machine operators, garment workers, textile-process trades, and production support in labor-intensive manufacturing.
canonical_rows.task_line: Runs repetitive but still tactile production steps, quality checks, and line-based coordination.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: Workflow planning and quality inspection will digitize further, but low wages and physical handling slow full automation.
sample_postings: Industrial tailor for export-garment stitching lines.
Textile production associate for inspection, folding, and packing coordination.

canonical_rows.title: Food and Related Products Machine Operators
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: Exposure is moderate because plant automation can advance, but the work still includes manual materials handling and shift-floor response.
canonical_rows.summary: Operates processing machinery in food manufacturing, packaging, and line-production settings.
canonical_rows.task_line: Monitors machines, handles materials, checks quality, and responds to production issues on the floor.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: Automation already exists in plants, but many Indian facilities still rely on mixed manual-machine workflows.
sample_postings: Packaging line operator for a snack-food manufacturing unit.
Process operator for dairy production and hygiene checks.

canonical_rows.title: Motor Vehicle Drivers
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: AI will change routing and dispatch systems a lot, but the actual driving task remains only moderately exposed in India.
canonical_rows.summary: Includes commercial drivers, delivery drivers, chauffeurs, and logistics vehicle operators.
canonical_rows.task_line: Drives in complex traffic conditions, handles loading or customer coordination, and manages route execution.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: Routing, fleet management, and dispatch are highly digitizable, but low-cost autonomous driving remains far from broad Indian deployment.
sample_postings: Last-mile delivery driver for e-commerce parcel routes.
Heavy vehicle driver for intercity logistics and loading coordination.

canonical_rows.title: Sales and Service Elementary Occupations
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: This group remains low exposure because much of the work is informal, physical, and tightly linked to local presence.
canonical_rows.summary: Represents low-formality sales and service support roles with heavy physical and interpersonal content.
canonical_rows.task_line: Runs errands, moves goods, supports service counters, and handles small operational tasks in person.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: Digital tools will reshape supervision and payments, but the jobs themselves often remain manually executed.
sample_postings: Helper role supporting retail dispatch and in-store customer flow.
Service assistant for field collection, errands, and local operations support.

canonical_rows.title: Agricultural, Fishery and Related Labourers
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: The work is overwhelmingly manual, local, and low-cost, so AI exposure is minimal.
canonical_rows.summary: Covers casual agricultural and allied labour performing field, fishery, and related manual tasks.
canonical_rows.task_line: Works on sowing, harvesting, carrying, sorting, feeding, and other labour-intensive operations.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: AI may improve advisory systems and farm planning, but this labor category faces minimal direct substitution from frontier models.
sample_postings: Farm labour hiring for harvest-season crop cutting and loading.
Fishery support work for net handling, sorting, and transport prep.

canonical_rows.title: Labourers in Mining, Construction, Manufacturing and Transport
canonical_rows.exposure_rationale: AI changes coordination around the job more than the core labor itself, so direct exposure remains low.
canonical_rows.summary: Includes helpers and manual labour roles around construction, manufacturing, warehousing, loading, and transport operations.
canonical_rows.task_line: Moves materials, supports trades, loads vehicles, handles simple tools, and works in unpredictable physical environments.
canonical_rows.ai_notes: Scheduling and supervision will digitize, but these occupations remain anchored in manual execution and low-cost labor.
sample_postings: Warehouse loader for FMCG dispatch and vehicle turnaround.
Construction helper supporting masons, bar-benders, and material movement.

display_nodes.summary: Aggregates 3 canonical NCO 2004 groups into this major group.

display_nodes.title: Directors and Chief Executives

display_nodes.title: Enterprise chief executives
display_nodes.summary: Leads whole companies or diversified business groups.

display_nodes.title: Operations and growth heads
display_nodes.summary: Runs scale, expansion, and cross-functional execution.

display_nodes.title: Business unit directors
display_nodes.summary: Owns category, regional, or line-of-business performance.

display_nodes.title: Other / General
display_nodes.summary: Residual share for general work inside the canonical occupation group.

display_nodes.title: Operations managers
display_nodes.summary: Coordinates staffing, workflow, schedules, and site execution.

display_nodes.title: Sales and category managers
display_nodes.summary: Owns channels, categories, and commercial targets.

display_nodes.title: HR and admin managers
display_nodes.summary: Handles people operations, policy, and facilities administration.

display_nodes.summary: Builds product features, services, and internal platforms.

display_nodes.title: Platform and data engineers
display_nodes.summary: Owns infrastructure, pipelines, and reliability tooling.

display_nodes.title: QA and automation engineers
display_nodes.summary: Automates testing, release checks, and quality workflows.

display_nodes.title: Civil engineers
display_nodes.summary: Designs roads, buildings, drainage, and project packages.

display_nodes.title: Mechanical and electrical engineers
display_nodes.summary: Works on plant systems, machines, and electrical assets.

display_nodes.title: Architects and design engineers
display_nodes.summary: Creates plans, drawings, and design specifications.

display_nodes.title: Physicians
display_nodes.summary: Diagnoses patients and makes treatment decisions.

display_nodes.title: Dentists and pharmacists
display_nodes.summary: Provides oral care or medicine-dispensing expertise.

display_nodes.title: Diagnostic specialists
display_nodes.summary: Interprets tests, scans, and specialist clinical evidence.

display_nodes.title: Hospital nurses
display_nodes.summary: Delivers bedside care, medication, and ward coordination.

display_nodes.summary: Supports ICU, emergency, and post-operative settings.

display_nodes.title: Community health nurses
display_nodes.summary: Handles outreach, maternal care, and field visits.

display_nodes.title: Other / General

display_nodes.title: School teachers
display_nodes.summary: Teaches classroom subjects in school settings.

display_nodes.title: Subject tutors
display_nodes.summary: Provides focused exam and subject coaching.

display_nodes.title: Skills and language instructors
display_nodes.summary: Trains spoken English, vocational, or employability skills.

display_nodes.title: Other / General
display_nodes.summary: Residual share for general work inside the canonical occupation group.

display_nodes.title: Accountants and auditors
display_nodes.summary: Prepares books, audits, and compliance reports.

display_nodes.title: Financial analysts and planners
display_nodes.summary: Builds forecasts, budgets, and business analysis.

display_nodes.title: HR and compliance specialists
display_nodes.summary: Owns policy, controls, hiring analytics, and governance.

display_nodes.title: IT support engineers
display_nodes.summary: Handles incidents, issue resolution, and user support.

display_nodes.title: System and network operators
display_nodes.summary: Monitors infrastructure, uptime, and operational systems.

display_nodes.title: ERP and implementation support
display_nodes.summary: Supports rollouts, tickets, and business-system configuration.

display_nodes.title: Executive assistants
display_nodes.summary: Manages calendars, meetings, and leadership coordination.

display_nodes.title: Office coordinators
display_nodes.summary: Runs records, correspondence, and workflow follow-ups.

display_nodes.title: Data-entry and document clerks
display_nodes.summary: Maintains digital forms, records, and repetitive documentation.

display_nodes.title: Billing clerks
display_nodes.summary: Prepares invoices, bills, and transactional records.

display_nodes.title: Accounts and payroll clerks
display_nodes.summary: Processes payroll, ledger entries, and reconciliations.

display_nodes.title: Inventory and record clerks
display_nodes.summary: Updates stock logs, dispatch records, and back-office counts.

display_nodes.title: Front-desk receptionists
display_nodes.summary: Greets visitors, manages appointments, and handles front desk flows.

display_nodes.title: Call-centre agents
display_nodes.summary: Resolves customer queries over phone and chat.

display_nodes.title: Customer-service desk clerks
display_nodes.summary: Handles service counters, complaint logging, and follow-up.

display_nodes.title: Housekeeping attendants
display_nodes.summary: Cleans rooms, facilities, and hospitality spaces.

display_nodes.title: Food-service stewards
display_nodes.summary: Supports restaurant floor and serving operations.

display_nodes.title: Catering helpers
display_nodes.summary: Assists bulk food service and event catering workflows.

display_nodes.title: Other / General
display_nodes.summary: Residual share for general work inside the canonical occupation group.

display_nodes.title: Childcare workers
display_nodes.summary: Looks after children in homes, schools, or centers.

display_nodes.title: Elder and patient attendants
display_nodes.summary: Supports mobility, hygiene, and routine care needs.

display_nodes.title: Beauty and wellness aides
display_nodes.summary: Provides salon, wellness, and personal-care assistance.

display_nodes.title: Other / General
display_nodes.summary: Residual share for general work inside the canonical occupation group.

display_nodes.title: Retail sales associates
display_nodes.summary: Sells goods in stores, showrooms, and branded outlets.

display_nodes.title: Counter sales staff
display_nodes.summary: Handles billing and walk-in product enquiries.

display_nodes.title: Field-promotion demonstrators
display_nodes.summary: Promotes products through demos and direct customer interaction.

display_nodes.title: Vegetable growers
display_nodes.summary: Grows seasonal vegetables for market sale.

display_nodes.title: Fruit growers
display_nodes.summary: Manages orchards and fruit production cycles.

display_nodes.title: Nursery growers
display_nodes.summary: Raises saplings, flowers, and nursery plants for sale.

display_nodes.title: Other / General
display_nodes.summary: Residual share for general work inside the canonical occupation group.

display_nodes.title: Dairy workers
display_nodes.summary: Manages milch animals, feeding, and milk collection.
